mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery, an accelerating rate of change in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals improving corporate health and future consumer demand. This leads to upward revisions of earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:NBER declared the Great Recession trough in June 2009, making recovery dynamics relevant.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive during the 24 months following an NBER-declared recession trough.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned near the zero lower bound, this highly accommodative monetary policy stance provides a strong tailwind for risk assets by lowering discount rates and forcing investors into higher-risk assets. This effect is only present when rates are exceptionally low.
evidence_refs: fact:The FOMC has initiated quantitative easing and signaled rates will remain low for an extended period.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects a strengthening supply side of the economy, indicating rising corporate revenues and operating leverage, which drives equity prices higher over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production has shown positive growth for two consecutive months, suggesting a bottoming process.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-year rank information coefficient between the 1-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When nominal retail sales growth (RSAFS) fails to keep pace with inflation (CPIAUCSL), real consumer spending is contracting. This signals a squeeze on corporate revenues and margins, leading to negative equity returns as earnings expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: 6-month % change in RSAFS < 6-month % change in CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A high but declining unemployment rate is a powerful positive signal for equities. The market prices in the full economic recovery well before unemployment returns to normal levels. The signal activates only when UNRATE is high but has clearly peaked.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment is at a multi-decade high, creating potential for a strong 'rate of change' signal once it inflects.
failure_condition: The average 60-day S&P 500 return following an instance of the trigger condition being met is not statistically greater than the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in housing starts from a deeply depressed level indicates a bottoming of the housing market. This stabilization removes a major source of systemic risk and signals renewed construction activity and wealth effects, boosting equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have risen from their April 2009 lows, suggesting a potential turn.
failure_condition: The signal (HOUST crossing above its 12-month moving average while below its 5-year average) does not lead to a positive information coefficient for 20-day forward returns over the subsequent 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals fundamental economic strength, leading to immediate upward revisions in aggregate earnings forecasts and positive short-term equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:The rate of GDP contraction slowed in Q2 2009, and markets are anticipating a return to positive growth.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return in the week following a positive QoQ GDPC1 release is not statistically different from the average return in the week following a negative release.

mechanism_narrative: In a ZIRP environment with high unemployment, a sharp uptick in inflation creates a policy dilemma for the central bank. Markets will fear a premature tightening to fight inflation that would choke off a fragile recovery, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month annualized change in CPIAUCSL > 2.5% AND UNRATE > 7.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Unprecedented monetary stimulus has created latent fears of future inflation.
failure_condition: During periods that meet the trigger condition, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive on average, indicating markets are not concerned about a policy error.
